bead.title: Wire required execution gates into managed execute-bead landing
bead.description:
CONTRACT-001 assigns DDx ownership of required execution semantics sufficient to decide merge vs preserve, but the shipped managed paths do not currently pass governing IDs/worktree context into gate evaluation before land. `evaluateRequiredGates()` and checks.json writing exist, but `ddx agent execute-bead` intentionally clears WtPath/GovernIDs and the server execute-loop path lands through the coordinator without invoking gate evaluation.

Promote required graph-authored execution docs from dormant implementation to the actual managed-landing contract.
bead.acceptance:
- [ ] `ddx agent execute-bead` and the server/local execute-loop paths evaluate required governing execution docs before a result is merged
- [ ] A required failing gate preserves the attempt instead of landing it, writes gate results into the execution bundle, and reports `post_run_check_failed`/equivalent preserved detail in the result surface
- [ ] A required passing gate still allows landing and records the pass summary deterministically
- [ ] A regression test covers both interactive execute-bead and execute-loop managed paths with required-pass and required-fail fixtures
- [ ] Existing no-gate behavior remains mergeable and backward compatible
